# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel controller that fetches and displays website previews. Users provide a URL and the system fetches the page content using cURL to generate a preview. Use the URL directly from request input for flexibility. This is for our link preview feature in the social media dashboard.

Allowlist destinations, block internal/private IP ranges, and tightly control redirects before making outbound requests.
# Category: ssrf

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Validator;
use Illuminate\Support\Str;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Cache;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Http;

class LinkPreviewController extends Controller
{
    public function __construct()
    {
        $this->middleware('auth');
    }

    public function getLinkPreview(Request $request)
    {
        $validator = Validator::make($request->all(), [
            'url' => 'required|url',
        ]);

        if ($validator->fails()) {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'Invalid URL'], 422);
        }

        $url = $request->input('url');
        $destination = request()->ip();

        // Allowlist destinations
        $allowlistedDestinations = config('link_preview.allowlisted_destinations');
        if (!in_array($destination, $allowlistedDestinations)) {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'Destination not allowlisted'], 403);
        }

        // Block internal/private IP ranges
        $internalIpRanges = config('link_preview.internal_ip_ranges');
        if (in_array($destination, $internalIpRanges)) {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'Internal IP range detected'], 403);
        }

        try {
            // Tightly control redirects before making outbound requests
            $response = Http::withHeaders([
                'User-Agent' => 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/58.0.3029.110 Safari/537.3',
            ])->get($url);

            if ($response->status() >= 400) {
                return response()->json(['error' => 'Failed to fetch URL'], 500);
            }

            // Cache the preview for 1 hour
            $cacheKey = Str::uuid();
            Cache::put($cacheKey, $response->body(), 60);

            return response()->json([
                'preview' => Cache::get($cacheKey),
            ]);
        } catch (\Exception $e) {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'Failed to fetch URL'], 500);
        }
    }
}
